cpt.description: Emergency department visit, high medical decision complexity

carc.description: Non-covered service — payer determined service not medically necessary.
carc.appealNotes: Frequently overturned when admitting diagnosis clinically supports the service. Cite ACEP or specialty guidelines. ER visits for infectious, cardiac, or neurologic diagnoses are almost always upheld. Request peer-to-peer review between your physician and the reviewing physician.

EXPLANATION OF BENEFITS
BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois
Member Services: 1-800-835-8699
PO Box 805107, Chicago, IL 60680

MEMBER INFORMATION
Member Name:      Jane Q. Smith
Member ID:        BCB-2204819334
Group Number:     GRP-00471920
Plan Name:        PPO Gold 1500
Claim Number:     48291
Date of Service:  June 14, 2026
Provider:         Memorial Hospital Emergency Department
Admitting Diagnosis: J18.9 - Pneumonia, unspecified organism

SERVICE LINES
Line  Date        Procedure  Description                     Billed    Allowed   Paid      Patient   Denial
1     06/14/2026  99285      ER visit, high complexity MDM   $1847.00  $0.00     $0.00     $0.00     CO-50, RARC N130
2     06/14/2026  71046      Chest X-ray 2 views             $612.00   $565.00   $489.00   $76.00    PR-1
3     06/14/2026  36415      Routine venipuncture            $45.00    $45.00    $36.00    $9.00     PR-1
4     06/14/2026  J0696      Ceftriaxone injection 250mg     $280.00   $0.00     $0.00     $0.00     CO-4

CLAIM TOTALS
Total Billed: $2784.00
Total Insurance Paid: $525.00
Total Patient Responsibility: $85.00

DENIAL CODE GLOSSARY
CO-50: Non-covered services - not deemed medically necessary by payer
CO-4: Procedure code inconsistent with modifier used or required modifier missing
PR-1: Deductible amount - patient responsibility

patientFacingSummary: Your insurer denied $2127 that may be wrongful — you have 2 appealable denials with a strong chance of reversal. We've drafted a ready-to-send appeal letter to help you recover this amount.
appealLetter:
**INTERNAL APPEAL — LEVEL 1**

Date: June 28, 2026

BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois
Appeals and Grievances Department
[Address]

**RE: Appeal of Denied Claim**
**Claim ID:** 48291
**Member ID:** BCB-2204819334
**Date of Service:** June 14, 2026
**Procedure Code:** 99285

Dear Appeals Committee:

I am writing to formally appeal the denial of the above-referenced claim. The denial was issued under code CO-50, stating the emergency department visit was "not medically necessary."

I respectfully dispute this determination. On June 14, 2026, I presented to Memorial Hospital Emergency Department with symptoms requiring urgent evaluation. The admitting diagnosis was J18.9 (Pneumonia, unspecified organism). Pneumonia is a potentially life-threatening respiratory infection that can rapidly progress to sepsis, respiratory failure, or death without prompt assessment and treatment. Emergency department evaluation for acute pneumonia symptoms—including diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and clinical assessment—represents the accepted standard of care per guidelines from the American Thoracic Society and Infectious Diseases Society of America. A high-complexity visit (99285) was clinically appropriate given the severity of this condition.

**Pursuant to my rights under ERISA §503 and applicable Illinois insurance regulations, I formally request the following information regarding the physician who reviewed and denied this claim:**
- Full name and professional title
- Medical license number and state(s) of licensure
- Board certifications and specialty area(s)
- Confirmation of whether they personally reviewed my complete medical record

A determination of medical necessity constitutes a clinical judgment that must be rendered by a qualified peer physician. If the reviewing physician's specialty does not correspond to the clinical nature of this service, such discrepancy constitutes additional grounds for overturning this denial.

I respectfully request Level-1 reconsideration and a written response within 30 days.

Sincerely,

Jane Q. Smith
Member ID: BCB-2204819334
